Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 2

1. Glossary
Applicant: Person applying for a Clarification.
Business Day: Any day of the week, except weekends and official holidays of the Federal
Government.
Clarification: An official document issued by the FTA in response to a request from a
specific Person regarding the Tax treatment of specific transactions.
Corporate Tax: The Tax imposed under Federal Decree-Law No. 47 of 2022 on Taxation
of Corporations and Businesses and its amendments, on juridical Persons and Business
income.
EmaraTax: The FTA’s core Tax system.
Excise Tax: Tax imposed under Federal Decree-Law No. 7 of 2017 on Excise Tax and
its amendments.
FTA: Federal Tax Authority.
FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025: Federal Tax Authority Decision No. 2 of 2025 on the
Authority’s Policy on Issuing Clarifications and Directives.
Legal Representative: The guardian or custodian of an incapacitated Person or minor,
or the bankruptcy trustee appointed by the court for a company that is in bankruptcy, or
any other Person legally appointed to represent another Person.
Person: A natural or juridical person.
Registrant: The Taxable Person who has been issued a Tax Registration Number.
Tax: Every Tax imposed under the Tax Law, that the FTA is mandated to administer,
collect and enforce, i.e. Corporate Tax, Excise Tax and VAT.
Tax Agent: Any Person registered with the FTA who is appointed on behalf of another
Person to represent him before the FTA and assist him in the fulfilment of his obligations
and the exercise of his associated Tax rights.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 3

Tax Law: Any federal law pursuant to which a Tax is imposed.
TRN: Tax Registration Number - unique number issued by the FTA to each Person who
is registered for Tax purposes.
Taxpayer: Any Person who is obligated to pay Tax in the UAE under the Tax Law whether
a Taxable Person or an end consumer.
VAT: Tax imposed under Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017 on Value Added Tax and its
amendments.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 4

2. Introduction
The FTA is the government entity responsible for the administration, collection, and
enforcement of federal Taxes in the United Arab Emirates (“UAE”).1
The following federal Taxes apply in the UAE:
• Excise Tax – introduced with effect from 1 October 2017.
• VAT – introduced with effect from 1 January 2018.
• Corporate Tax – introduced with effect from 1 June 2023.
The FTA publishes guides, public clarifications, and other information to help Taxpayers
understand their Tax obligations and to support voluntary compliance with the UAE Tax
laws. For other Tax matters of uncertainty that are not covered under the guides, public
clarifications and other publications issued by the FTA, Taxpayers may seek guidance
from the FTA on specific Tax matters through the private clarification mechanism which
is available via EmaraTax.
2.1. Purpose of this guide
The purpose of this document is to provide guidance on:
• the nature of a Clarification,
• who is eligible to apply for a Clarification,
• the instances where a Clarification will not be provided, or rejected,
• the Clarification process, including required documents, applicable fees and the
instances in which the fees may be refunded.
For more details on the application process via EmaraTax, please refer to the service
card which is available on the FTA’s website at
https://tax.gov.ae/en/services/tax.clarifications.request.aspx.
1 Article 4(1) of the FTA Law.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 5

2.2. Legislative references
In this guide:
• Federal Decree-Law No. 13 of 2016 on the Establishment of the Federal Tax Authority,
and its amendments, is referred to as “FTA Law”.
• Federal Decree-Law No. 28 of 2022 on Tax Procedures, and its amendments, is
referred to as “Tax Procedures Law”.
• Cabinet Decision No. 65 of 2020 on Fees for the Services Provided by the Federal
Tax Authority, and its amendments, is referred to as “Cabinet Decision No. 65 of 2020”.
• Federal Tax Authority Decision No. 5 of 2024 on the Refund of Fees of Private
Clarification Requests is referred to as “FTA Decision No. 5 of 2024”.
• Federal Tax Authority Decision No. 2 of 2025 on the Authority’s Policy on Clarifications
and Directives is referred to as “FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025”.
2.3. Status of the guide
This guide is not a legally binding document, but is intended to assist in understanding
when a Person may apply for a Clarification. The information provided in this guide should
not be interpreted as legal or Tax advice. It is not meant to be comprehensive and does
not provide a definitive answer in every case. It is based on the legislation as it stood
when the guide was published. Each Person’s own specific circumstances should be
considered.
Nothing in this guide modifies, or is intended to modify, the requirements of any legislation.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 6

3. Nature of Clarifications
Private clarifications are Clarifications issued by the FTA in the form of a document that
is stamped and signed by the Director General or his representative/delegate, in relation
to specific Tax technical matters. These documents are issued to a specific Taxpayer,
based on the Clarification request submitted on EmaraTax and the documents attached
to the request.2
Clarifications set out the relevant Tax legislation and application thereof to the specific
background facts provided by the Applicant. The FTA’s response is based solely on the
information and documents provided by the Applicant. The FTA will not verify the specific
facts provided by the Applicant, but may request further information, if needed.
The Clarification provided by the FTA is only applicable to the specific Applicant in respect
of the specific questions asked based on the facts provided for the specific Tax type
indicated. Clarifications should not be used by any Person other than the Applicant. The
FTA considers itself administratively bound to follow the position stated in the Clarification
if the factual circumstances are materially the same as set out in the Clarification request.
Issued Clarifications are void ab initio (i.e. treated as if not issued) if:
• any facts stated in the Clarification request (or any other facts subsequently provided
in response to a request for further information) are different from the transaction
actually carried out,
• there is fraud, misrepresentation or non-disclosure of a material fact, or
• any condition or assumption stipulated by the FTA in the Clarification is not satisfied
or carried out.
Furthermore, the Clarification will cease to be effective if the provisions of the Tax
legislation that are the subject thereof are repealed or amended, or a public clarification
or guide is later issued on the same subject following the issuance of the Clarification. In
such cases, the FTA is not obliged to notify the Applicant or to otherwise publish a notice
of withdrawal or modification of the Clarification. The onus remains on the Applicant to
stay up to date on changes in Tax Laws and guidance published on the FTA’s website.
2 FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 7

The FTA may withdraw a Clarification. In such instance, the Applicant may rely on the
issued Clarification until the effective date of the withdrawal. If the FTA issues a
subsequent Clarification to the Taxpayer on the same subject, the subsequent
Clarification shall supersede the previous Clarification with effect from the date specified
in the new Clarification.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 8

4. Eligibility criteria
There are two types of eligibility criteria to consider, firstly whether the relevant Person is
eligible to submit a Clarification request and secondly whether the specific request is
eligible to be considered under the Clarification process.
4.1. Eligible Persons
The Person seeking clarification on a Tax matter of uncertainty may apply for a
Clarification.
In the case of a Tax group, only the representative member of that Tax group is permitted
to request the Clarification, and this must be submitted through the EmaraTax account
and TRN of the Tax group, i.e. it should not be requested through the EmaraTax account
or TRN of the representative member or any other members of the Tax group.
It is important to note that there are different types of Tax groups, e.g. VAT and Corporate
Tax groups. The representative member of a VAT group may not submit a Corporate Tax
Clarification request in respect of a member of its VAT group, unless the Persons are part
of the same Corporate Tax group and the Person is also the parent company of that
Corporate Tax group. Similarly, the representative member of a Corporate Tax group may
not submit a VAT Clarification request in respect of a member of its Corporate Tax group,
unless the Persons are part of the same VAT group and the Applicant is also the
representative member of that VAT group.
Tax Agents and Legal Representatives
The Person’s (or Tax group’s) Tax Agent or Legal Representative may apply on behalf of
the Person.
Note that only a Tax Agent registered with the FTA for the specific Tax type the request
relates to, may submit the Clarification request on behalf of the Person. In such case, the
name of the relevant Taxpayer should be reflected on the Clarification request, i.e. the
request must be submitted in the name of the Taxpayer with the Taxpayer’s TRN, if
applicable.
Clarification requests submitted by Tax advisors/consultants or any other Person that is
not the Taxpayer’s Tax Agent or Legal Representative will not be accepted.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 9

Tax affairs of another Person
Clarifications will only address the Tax matters of uncertainty of the Applicant and not the
Tax affairs of any other Person. For example, the Recipient of Goods or the Recipient of
Services may not apply for a Clarification of the Tax treatment to be applied by the
supplier of the Goods or Services. In such instance, the supplier may submit its own
Clarification request if the eligibility criteria are met.
In exceptional cases, more than one Person may submit a joint Clarification request, e.g.
where they are parties to the same transaction and the factual background information is
agreed between them. These instances will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis to
determine whether the parties may be co-Applicants to the same Clarification request.3
Corporate Tax Clarifications
For Corporate Tax, only Applicants registered for Corporate Tax are eligible to apply for
a Clarification, unless the Clarification request relates to registration, or is submitted by
an Exempt Person as determined under Article 4(1) of the Corporate Tax Law.
Excise Tax and VAT Clarifications
Taxpayers are eligible to apply for these Clarifications, regardless of whether the Person
is registered for the specific Tax type or not, provided that it is an eligible matter as
clarified in section 4.2.
4.2. Eligible matters
Eligible Persons (as clarified in section 4.1) may only apply for a Clarification if there is a
specific Tax matter(s) of uncertainty.
The Taxpayer (or its authorised signatory, Tax Agent, Legal Representative) may only
submit a Clarification request if the following requirements are met:
• The request relates to federal Taxes or relevant administrative penalties, but not the
waiver thereof.
• The request clearly indicates the relevant Tax type the request relates to.
3 FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 10

• The request relates to Tax legislation as applied to the facts and circumstances of the
Taxpayer (or in exceptional cases, Taxpayers) submitting the request, i.e. the
Clarification issued by the FTA is not in respect of a third party.
• The request contains all the relevant information the FTA needs to consider for
deciding on the correct Tax treatment of the subject of the request.4
If all of the above requirements are not met, the FTA may reject the request for a
Clarification.
4.3. Grounds for rejection – Clarification requests that will be rejected
The FTA will not accept a Clarification request in the following instances:
4.3.1 Applicant is not eligible to submit the Clarification request
The Clarification request is submitted by:
• A Person representing the Applicant (e.g. the authorised signatory) but the relevant
proof of authorisation is not provided.
• A Tax Agent/Legal Representative but the request does not include the Taxpayer’s
details, such as the Taxpayer’s name and TRN relating to the Tax type the Clarification
is submitted for, unless the Taxpayer is not registered for that Tax type.
• A Tax Agent that is not registered with the FTA for the Tax type the Clarification
request relates to, for example, a Corporate Tax Clarification request submitted by a
Tax Agent that is only registered for indirect Tax (i.e. Excise Tax and VAT).
• The Applicant requests a Clarification relating to Corporate Tax but is not registered
for Corporate Tax, unless the Clarification request relates to registration or is
submitted by an Exempt Person as determined under Article 4(1) of the Corporate
Tax Law.
• A natural Person is reflected as the Applicant but the Clarification request pertains to
a juridical Person.
• A member of the relevant Tax group, other than the representative member/parent
company of that Tax group using the EmaraTax account and TRN of the Tax group.
• A Tax advisor or any other person on behalf of the Taxpayer if that Person is not
registered with the FTA as a Tax Agent, or is not appointed as the Taxpayer’s Legal
Representative.
4 FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 11

The Applicant should submit the Clarification request using its own profile on EmaraTax
(or via a Tax Agent). If the Applicant is registered for the relevant Tax type and does not
submit the Clarification request via its own EmaraTax profile, the request shall be rejected.
4.3.2 Out of scope cases
The FTA will reject Clarification requests submitted for the following, as these fall outside
the scope of the Clarification process:
• Administrative exceptions.
• Administrative Penalties - Payment in instalments and waiver requests.
• Advance pricing agreements.
• Apportionment - Request to use a special apportionment method and/or a specified
recovery percentage.
• Commercial activity certificate.
• IT System issues/queries.
• Pillar Two (GloBE Rules). The date on which the Clarification can be submitted on the
Imposition of Top-up Tax on Multinational Enterprises will be announced at a later
stage.
• Reconsideration.
• Tax assessment review.
• Tax residency certificate (“TRC”), unless the request relates to whether a Person is
eligible to apply for a TRC.
Many of the above cases fall outside the scope of the Clarification service due to the
availability of an alternative service specifically for that type of request. These services
can be accessed via the FTA’s website at:
https://tax.gov.ae//en/services.aspx?type=3&typename=customr.service
4.3.3 Applicant did not respond to additional information request
The FTA will reject the Clarification request if it requested additional information or
documents from the Applicant, and the Applicant failed to submit all the requested
information or documents within 40 Business Days from the date the request for
information was sent to the Applicant.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 12

4.4 Grounds for rejection – Clarification requests that may be rejected
The FTA may reject a Clarification request in the following instances:
4.4.1 Incomplete or incorrect Clarification requests
The FTA may either request further information or reject a Clarification request if the
request (e.g. submission via EmaraTax) is not correctly completed, or is incomplete. This
includes, for example, the following cases:
• All relevant information in respect of the specific Tax matter are not provided.
• Sufficient Tax technical analysis is not provided – a clear analysis in relation to the
specific Tax matter must be provided, including references to legal provisions and
guidance which are relevant to assessing the Tax treatment of the matter, as well as
Tax advice obtained from Tax Agents/advisors/consultants.
• An alternative Tax technical analysis is not provided – the lack of an alternative
analysis may indicate that there is no genuine point of uncertainty and that the
Applicant is merely seeking confirmation of their Tax position.
• Relevant supporting documents are not provided - documentary proof to support the
information provided and Tax technical analysis is required, for example, sample
invoices, contracts, payment slips, Tax advice received, etc.
• Some of the fields in the Clarification request form are incomplete – it is not sufficient
to merely refer to an attached letter, state that the question is not applicable, or repeat
the same response in multiple fields. All fields must be completed with sufficient
relevant detail to enable the FTA to address the Tax matter of uncertainty.
• There is a discrepancy between the information provided in the EmaraTax Clarification
request and an attached document. Information reflected on the request form should
align with the supporting documents uploaded.
• The Clarification request does not relate to the correct Tax type(s).
4.4.2 Cases that do not represent a Tax matter of uncertainty
Taxpayers are required to consider the relevant guidance already provided by the FTA
before applying for a Clarification. The FTA may reject a Clarification request if the specific
Tax matter was already previously clarified, for example:
• An FTA guide or public clarification already addresses the specific Tax matter the
Applicant is requesting a Clarification on.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 13

• A Clarification on the same specific Tax matter was previously issued to the same
Applicant.
In addition, the FTA may reject Clarification requests where no specific matter of Tax
uncertainty has been identified and the Applicant is merely asking for confirmation of their
position or status for Tax purposes. This includes the following:
• Confirmation that the Applicant meets all or any of the conditions to be a Qualifying
Free Zone Person, including whether the Applicant is located in a Free Zone or
Designated Zone for Corporate Tax purposes, conducting a Qualifying Activity and
maintaining adequate substance.5
• Confirmation that the Applicant is a Government Entity or Charity for VAT purposes,
or Government Controlled Entity or Qualifying Public Benefit Entity for Corporate Tax
purposes.
• Confirmation that the Applicant has an Extractive or Non-Extractive Natural Resource
Business. A Person claiming this status is required to submit a notification to the
Ministry of Finance.6
• Confirmation that the Applicant is an Exempt Person under Article 4(1)(f), (g), (h) or (i)
of the Corporate Tax Law. Such Persons are required to submit an application to the
FTA.
• Confirmation that the Applicant is eligible to form/amend/leave a Tax group, change
their Tax Period, or amend their Tax Registration details. Such Persons are required
to submit an application to the FTA.
4.4.3 Tax Audits and Tax Assessments
The FTA may reject Clarification requests if:
• A Tax Assessment on the same specific Tax matter was previously issued to the same
Applicant.
• The Applicant is subject to a Tax Audit, Tax Assessment or inspection by the FTA,
and the subject matter of the Clarification request is related to a matter under Tax
Audit, Tax Assessment or inspection.
4.4.4 Other cases
In addition to the above, the FTA may also reject Clarification requests if:
5 Article 18 of the Corporate Tax Law.
6 Article 7 and Article 8 of the Corporate Tax Law.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 14

• The Clarification request is based on a hypothetical scenario that has not been
seriously considered by the Applicant. The Clarification service is not an advisory
service and the FTA will, generally, not address hypothetical scenarios.
• The Applicant is requesting Tax advice from the FTA. For example, the FTA cannot
advise an Applicant on what steps they are required to take in order to be a Qualifying
Free Zone Person.
• The Applicant is asking the FTA to confirm a matter of fact that can only be established
during a Tax Audit. For example, the FTA cannot confirm that an Applicant has
adequate substance.
• The Applicant is asking the FTA to confirm that a transaction or arrangement between
Related Parties meet the arm’s length standard or are at Market Value. For example,
the FTA cannot confirm that the interest rate on an intra-group loan is at arm’s length.
This can only be confirmed through the conclusion of an advance pricing agreement.
In line with FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025, applications for unilateral advance pricing
agreements shall be received from the 4th quarter of 2025. The date of receiving any
other advance pricing agreement applications shall be announced after that date.
• The Clarification request relates to more than one Tax but is not in respect of the same
specific Tax matter. For example, a Registrant may submit a single Clarification
request to cover the VAT and Corporate Tax treatment of the same group restructuring.
However, if the request covers different matters, such as the VAT treatment of
employee benefits and whether distribution activities constitute qualifying activities
from a Corporate Tax perspective, the Taxpayer would need to submit two separate
Clarification requests.
• The Clarification request is in relation to an administrative process, e.g. process for
amending the Applicant’s Tax Registration details, how to make certain elections, how
to apply for a Tax Residency Certificate or how to apply for a waiver of Administrative
Penalties.
• The Clarification request addresses issues that the FTA suspects may constitute Tax
planning, Tax avoidance or Tax evasion, including the application of Article 50 of the
Corporate Tax Law regarding the General Anti-abuse Rules or similar provisions
under double taxation agreements.
In the case of a rejection, the Clarification request fee would be forfeited unless one of
exceptions stated in section 6.1 applies.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 15

5. Clarification process
Clarification requests are only accepted via EmaraTax. In order to submit a request, the
Applicant must have a user profile. For more information on the process to create a user
profile, please refer to the EmaraTax Register as an Online User – User Manual which is
available on the FTA’s website.
5.1 Applying for a Clarification
The Applicant may request a Clarification in English or Arabic. Clarifications are issued in
the same language the Clarification request is submitted in, i.e. if the request is submitted
in Arabic, the Clarification will be issued in Arabic. It is, therefore, important that the
Applicant decides whether they prefer the Clarification to be issued in Arabic or English
before starting the Clarification request process.
5.1.1 Completing the Clarification request form
To submit a Clarification request, the Applicant should select the “Tax Clarification
Request” tile on the FTA’s website:
https://tax.gov.ae/en/services/tax.clarifications.request.aspx
The Applicant will then be requested to log into their profile to proceed with the
Clarification request. Where the Applicant is already registered for the relevant Tax, the
Applicant should select the “Taxable Person” tile in their account before proceeding to
submit a Clarification request. If the Person is not registered for the Tax type and is an
Eligible Person (as clarified in section 4.1), the Person would need to create a user profile
on EmaraTax to access the Clarification service.
All of the mandatory fields of the request form must be completed with sufficient detail.
The following supporting evidence should be uploaded as part of the request:
• A cover letter that describes the background facts, the specific Tax technical query,
relevant UAE Tax legislation considered, the Applicant’s technical view as well as any
alternative Tax treatment.
• Documentary evidence relating to the request, for example, contracts, invoices,
correspondence, etc.
• Any Tax advice received from third parties in respect of the specific matter the
Clarification is requested on.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 16

If the Clarification request relates to more than one Tax, all of the above need to be
provided for each of the relevant Taxes the Clarification relates to. The documents can
be uploaded in Excel, JPEG, JPG, PDF and PNG format. The individual file size limit is
5 MB.
5.1.2 Clarification request fee
The fee for applying for a Clarification in respect of a single Tax is AED 1,500 per
application. If the Clarification request relates to more than one Tax, the fee is AED 2,250
per application.7 These fees are non-refundable, except for the cases listed in FTA
Decision No. 5 of 2024 (see section 6.16.1 in this regard). The fees can only be paid via
the EmaraTax portal using a valid bank card.
5.1.3 Submitting the Clarification request
The Applicant can save draft versions of the request. However, the request must be
submitted within 40 Business Days from the date the Applicant initiated the Clarification
request on EmaraTax, otherwise the request will be closed.
Once the request is submitted and the relevant fee is paid, a unique Clarification request
refence number will be issued. This reference number as well as the relevant TRN, if
applicable, should be reflected in all correspondence with the FTA relating to the specific
Clarification request.
Requests received after 3pm on a Business Day will be regarded as being received on
the following Business Day. For example, if a request is received at 3:30pm on a Friday,
the request will be regarded as being received on the following Monday, assuming the
Monday is not a federal public holiday.
5.1.4 Withdrawal of a Clarification request
Applicants are allowed to withdraw their Clarification request, but the Clarification request
fee would be forfeited unless the request is withdrawn within two Business Days from the
date the request was submitted.8
7 Cabinet Decision No. 65 of 2020.
8 Article 2(1) of FTA Decision No. 5 of 2024.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 17

5.2 Validation of eligibility
The FTA will review the Clarification request to determine whether the Applicant is eligible
to submit the Clarification request and that none of the grounds for rejection listed in
section 4.3 apply. The FTA will also consider whether any of the grounds of rejection in
section 4.4 apply, and whether the Clarification request will be rejected or accepted. The
Applicant will be notified if the Clarification request is rejected and reasons will be
provided for such rejection via EmaraTax.
If the Clarification request is accepted, the FTA may request further information. If further
information is requested, the Applicant is required to submit the requested information
within 40 Business Days from the date the information was requested. If the Applicant
does not provide the requested information within this period, the Application request will
be rejected.9
5.3 Issuance of Clarifications
Clarifications are issued in either Arabic or English, depending on the Applicant’s
preference. If the Clarification request was submitted in Arabic, the Clarification will also
be issued in Arabic, whereas the Clarification would be issued in English if the request
was submitted in English.
In the case of Clarification requests relating to more than one Tax type, separate
Clarifications will be issued for each Tax type covered under the request.
Clarifications will be issued within 60 Business Days from the date the request was
received. If further information was requested, the Clarification will be issued within 60
Business Days from the date the further information was received.
In the case of complex Tax matters, e.g. where other stakeholders need to be consulted,
the FTA may request an extension for issuing the relevant Clarification.
9 FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 18

6. Other matters relating to Clarifications
6.1 Refunding Clarification fees
The FTA may refund the fee paid for a Clarification request relating to one Tax or more
Taxes, if the FTA decided not to issue the Clarification provided any of the following cases
apply:10
• The Applicant withdraws the Clarification request within two Business Days from the
date the request was submitted.
• The Applicant is not registered for Corporate Tax and the Clarification request relates
to a Corporate Tax matter other than registration.
• The Applicant is subject to a Tax Audit at the time of submitting the Clarification
request.
• The Clarification request relates to procedures that should be applied as a result of an
FTA Decision.
• The Clarification request is a duplicate of another request submitted by the same
Applicant with the same subject and documents submitted, if the FTA is already
working on the other submitted request.
• The Clarification request is related to a matter the FTA is coordinating with the Ministry
of Finance on to amend Tax legislation regulating it.11 In such instance the Applicant
may resubmit a Clarification request after the Tax legislation is amended, provided
that there is still a Tax matter of uncertainty.
In the above instances, the Clarification fee will be refunded automatically, i.e. the
Applicant need not lodge a separate refund application.
In any other instance, e.g. if a Clarification request is submitted on a matter already
clarified in a public clarification or guide, or the request is withdrawn after more than two
Business Days, the Clarification fee will not be refunded to the Applicant.
Extent of Clarification fee refund
The extent of the refund is determined based on the number of Taxes covered under the
Clarification request. If any of the above cases applies, the extent of the refund will be as
10 Article 1(1) of FTA Decision No. 5 of 2024.
11 Article 2 of FTA Decision No. 5 of 2024.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 19

follows:
• If the Clarification request related to a single Tax, the full amount of the service fee of
AED 1,500 will be refunded.12
• If the Clarification request related to more than one Tax and the FTA does not issue
any Clarification in respect of that request, the full amount of the service fee of
AED 2,250 will be refunded.13
• If the Clarification request related to more than one Tax and the FTA issues a
Clarification on one of the Taxes, only AED 750 will be refunded, i.e. the difference
between the service fee of a Clarification request relating to more than one Tax
(AED 2,250) and the service fee of a Clarification relating to one Tax (AED 1,500).14
6.2 Disagreeing with Clarification issued
A Clarification is not considered to be a decision that is issued by the FTA and, therefore,
is not subject to the dispute resolution process. Consequently, Applicants cannot apply
for a reconsideration or review of a Clarification.
The Applicant may, however, apply for a new Clarification if the factual circumstances are
materially different from what was submitted as part of the previous request for
Clarification, or where the Applicant has new information which may change the outcome
of the Clarification.
12 Article 1(2)(a) of FTA Decision No. 5. of 2024, read with Cabinet Decision No. 65 of 2020.
13 Article 1(2)(b) of FTA Decision No. 5. of 2024, read with Cabinet Decision No. 65 of 2020.
14 Article 1(2)(c) of FTA Decision No. 5 of 2024, read with Cabinet Decision No. 65 of 2020.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 20

7. Common errors
The FTA identified the following common errors based on Clarification requests received,
which led to either rejection of the request or a request for further information:
• Clarification requests submitted in the name of a natural Person while the request
pertains to a legal Person.
• Clarification requests submitted by members of a Tax group, other than the
representative member (for VAT) or parent company (for Corporate Tax) of that Tax
group using the EmaraTax account and TRN of the Tax group.
• Clarification requests submitted by Tax advisors and other Persons who are not Tax
Agents, on behalf of a Taxpayer.
• Incomplete Clarification request forms – it is not sufficient to merely refer to an
attached letter, state that the question is not applicable, or repeat the same response
in multiple fields. All fields must be completed with sufficient detail to allow the FTA to
answer the Clarification request.
• Failure to submit a cover letter (as further explained below).
• Insufficient background information/supporting information provided.
• Contradictory information reflected in the Clarification Request form, cover letter and
other supporting documents, e.g. contracts.
• Failure to provide a Tax technical analysis and application of the Tax law, as well as
alternative Tax treatments.
• Clarification requests relying on non-UAE Tax laws, e.g. GCC Agreements or Tax laws
of other countries.
• Clarification requests submitted to request administrative exceptions,
reconsiderations, refunds, waiver of Administrative Penalties or for special exceptions
to disregard/override provisions of the Tax Law.
• Clarification requests submitted in respect of matters already clarified in FTA guides,
public clarifications or Clarifications issued to the same Applicant on the same matter.
• Clarification requests that ask the FTA to confirm whether the Applicant has applied
the correct Tax treatment, without specifying exactly what the Tax matter of
uncertainty is.
• Clarification requests that ask the FTA to confirm that the Applicant is eligible for
certain reliefs or exemptions, or that the Applicant meets certain conditions, without
specifying exactly what the Tax matter of uncertainty is.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 21

Cover letters
Applicants may consider structuring their cover letter around the following headings to
ensure that all the relevant information is submitted as part of the Clarification request:
• Background information
o The background information should be complete with all the details needed for the
FTA to determine the appropriate Tax treatment. No additional background
information should be reflected in the analysis of the Tax matter.
o The relevant transaction should be clearly described, e.g. the supplier and
recipient should be clearly identified, whether it is a one-off transaction or
continuous in nature and whether the request is in respect of a past, current or
future transaction.
o A description should be provided of, and reference to other supporting documents
uploaded as part of the Clarification request, to explain their relevance to the
Clarification request. In the case of long agreements/contracts, references should
be made to the relevant clauses to consider.
• Request
o The request should be clear and concise, focusing on the exact Tax matter of
uncertainty the Applicant is requesting a Clarification on.
o If there is more than one question, the questions should be clearly numbered and
based on the background information required.
• Legislation and guidance considered
o The relevant Tax legislation considered as well as the applicable Articles, Clauses
and Paragraphs should be listed.
o The relevant FTA guides and public clarifications which were considered before
the Clarification request was submitted, should be referred to.
• Analysis of the matter and technical assessment
o A detailed Tax technical analysis based on the background information submitted
as part of the Clarification request and the applicable UAE Tax legislation, should
be provided.
o No new background information should be reflected in this section.
o Based on the Tax technical analysis, the Applicant should clearly state its
conclusion, i.e. the Applicant’s view on the correct Tax treatment of the matter of
uncertainty.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 22

• Alternative Tax treatment
o Since Clarifications are only issued in respect of Tax matters of uncertainty, it
implies that there is an alternative Tax treatment for the specific matter.
o In this section, the Applicant should provide a detailed analysis of any other
alternative Tax treatment, and explain why the alternative Tax treatment is less
appropriate than the Applicant’s proposed Tax treatment reflected in the previous
section.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 23

8. Updates and amendments
Date of amendment Amendments made
December 2022 • Revised the whole guide for simplification and amendment of
content in light of recent updates.
• Addition of section 7 to clarify that Clarifications do not
constitute an official decision by the FTA and are, therefore, not
subject to the dispute resolution process.
June 2023 • Revised the whole guide for simplification and amendment of
content in light of recent updates.
• Removed references to eServices.
• Updated description of fields in Clarification application as per
EmaraTax.
• Addition of a section on fees for submitting Clarification
requests.
November 2024 • Revised the whole guide for simplification and amendment of
content in light of recent updates.
• Added information regarding Corporate Tax Clarification
requests and confirmed that only persons registered for
Corporate Tax may apply for Clarifications, unless the request
relates to eligibility to register for Corporate Tax.
• Updated to incorporate FTA Decisions No. 4 and 5 of 2024.
• Removed description fields relating to EmaraTax application
fields.
• Added a list of common errors and outline for cover letters.
• Added a glossary for commonly used terms.
July 2025 • Updated to incorporate FTA Decision No. 2 of 2025.
• Added information regarding Pillar Two (GloBE Rules)
Clarification requests.
• Updated the period within which Tax Clarifications are issued.
• Updated the list of grounds for rejecting Clarification requests
to distinguish between instances where Clarification requests
will be rejected from instances where Clarification requests
may be rejected.
Private Clarifications | Tax Procedures | TPGPC1 24

title: Concerned Services – Accounting for Output Tax, issuing Tax Invoices, and Input Tax recovery Issue
text:
VATP044
VAT Public Clarification ة فاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأشب ماع حيضوت
Concerned Services – Accounting for Output Tax, رادصإو تاجرخملا ةبيرض باستحا – ةينعملا تامدخلا
issuing Tax Invoices, and Input Tax recovery تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتساو ةيبيرضلا ريتاوفلا
Issue عوضوملا
Taxable Persons receiving Services from outside نم تامدخ نوقلتي نيذلا ةبيرضلل نيعضاخلا ىلع نيعتي
the UAE while the place of supply of such Services باستحا ،ةلودلا يف اهديروت ناكم نوكي امدنع ،ةلودلا جراخ
is in the UAE, are required to account for VAT on ام كلذو ،ةينعملا تامدخلا كلت ىلع ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض
these Concerned Services, unless the Services . ةلودلا يف اهديروت مّ ت لاح يف ةافعمُ تامدخلا نكت مل
would be exempt had they been supplied in the
UAE.
This Public Clarification clarifies the requirement to باستحاب صّ اخلا بلطتملا نايب ىلإ مّ اعلا حيضوتلا اذه فدهي
account for the Output Tax and issue Tax Invoices قلعتي اميف ةيبيرضلا ريتاوفلا رادصإو تاجرخملا ةبيرض
in respect of Concerned Services and the ةبيرض دادرتسلا ةيدنتسملا تابلطتملاو ةينعملا تامدخلاب
documentary requirements for recovering Input .تامدخلا هذهب قلعتي اميف تلاخدملا
Tax in respect of these Services.
Summary صخلم
Taxable Persons that Import Concerned Services داريتساب نوموقي نيذلا ةبيرضلل نيعضاخلا ىلإ رظنُي
from outside the UAE, which have a place of supply اهديروت ناكم نوكي يتلاو ،ةلودلا جراخ نم ةينعم تامدخ
in the UAE, are regarded as making Taxable ةبيرضلل ةعضاخ تاديروتب نوموقي مهنأ ىلع ،ةلودلا لخاد
Supplies to themselves, unless the Services would مّ ت لاح يف ةافعمُ تامدخلا هذه نكت مل ام كلذو ،مهتاذل
be exempt had they been supplied in the UAE. . ةلودلا لخاد اهديروت
A Registrant receiving the Concerned Service )"يقلتملا" ( ةينعملا ةمدخلا ىقلتي يذلا لجسمُ لا ىلع نيعتي
(“Recipient”) is required to account for Output Tax ةينعملا ةمدخلاب ةقلعتملا تاجرخملا ةبيرض باستحا
in respect of the Concerned Service and to issue a دق يقلتملا نكي مل ام كلذو ،هتاذل ةيبيرض ريتاوف رادصإو
Tax Invoice to itself, unless the Recipient obtained رادصإ مدع هل زيجُي ةئيهلا نم يرادإ ءانثتسا ىلع لصح
an administrative exception from the FTA allowing .ةيبيرض ةروتاف
them not to issue a Tax Invoice.
1/9

A Recipient may recover the related Input Tax to ردقلاب ةلصلا تاذ تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتسا يقلتملل زوجي
the extent the Concerned Service was acquired to تاديروتب مايقلل ةينعملا تامدخلا ىلع هيف لصحي يذلا
make Taxable Supplies, provided the Recipient is ةبيرضل ًلاجسمُ يقلتملا نوكي نأ ةطيرش ،ةبيرضلل ةعضاخ
registered for VAT and obtains and retains the تاذ ةمعادلا تادنتسملا ىلع لصحي نأو ةفاضملا ةميقلا
relevant supporting documents, e.g. the supplier’s .د رّ وملا ةروتاف لثم ،اهب ظفتحيو ةلصلا
invoice.
Detailed analysis يليصفت ليلحت
General ّم اع
In this clarification, Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of مقر يداحتا نوناقب موسرملا ىلإ راشُي ،حيضوتلا اذه يف
2017 on Value Added Tax and its amendments is هتلايدعتو ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأش يف 2017 ةنسل )8(
referred to as “Decree-Law” and Cabinet Decision مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق ىلإو ،)"نوناقب موسرملا") ةرابعب
No. 52 of 2017 on the Executive Regulation of the نوناقب موسرملل ةيذيفنتلا ةحئلالا نأش يف 2017 ةنسل )52(
Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017 on Value Added ةميقلا ةبيرض نأش يف 2017 ةنسل )8( مقر يداحتا
Tax, and its amendments, is referred to as .)"ةيذيفنتلا ةحئلالا"( ةرابعب هتلايدعتو ةفاضملا
“Executive Regulation”.
A Registrant1 making Taxable Supplies is required ةبيرضلل ةعضاخ تاديروتب موقي يذلا 1لجسملا ىلع نيعتي
to report the VAT imposed on the supply and issue ديروتلا ىلع ةضورفملا ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نع رارقلإا
an original Tax Invoice within 14 days from the date خيرات نم ً اموي 14 للاخ ةيلصأ ةيبيرض ةروتاف رادصإو
of supply.2 2.ديروتلا
A Registrant is only entitled to recover the related دنع طقف ةينعملا تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتسا لجسمُ لل قحي
Input Tax when all the relevant requirements are ظافتحلاا كلذ يف امب ،ةلصلا تاذ تابلطتملا عيمج ءافيتسا
met, including the retention of the relevant . ةلصلا تاذ ةمعادلا تادنتسملاب
supporting documents.
These distinct matters are addressed in more detail . هاندأ ليصفتلا نم دٍ يزمب ةفلتخملا لئاسملا هذه لوانت متي
below.
2/9

Obligation to account for Output Tax تاجرخملا ةبيرض باستحاب ةقلعتملا تامازتللاا
Taxable Persons often receive Services from .ةلودلا جراخ نم تامدخ ةبيرضلل نوعضاخلا ىقلتي ام ًابلاغ
outside the UAE. Where the place of supply of نإف ،ةلودلا لخاد تامدخلا كلت ديروت ناكم نوكي امدنعو
these services is in the UAE, such imported Services لا نأ ةطيرش ،ةينعم تامدخ لكشُت ةدروتسملا تامدخلا هذه
constitute Concerned Services, provided they اهديروت مّ ت لاح يف ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نم ةافعمُ نوكت
would not be exempt from VAT had they been 1.ةلودلا يف
supplied in the UAE.1
The Taxable Person is regarded as making a عضاخ ديروتب موقي هنأ ىلع ةبيرضلل عضاخلا ىلإ رظنُي
Taxable Supply to itself when it imports a ، يلاتلابو 3.ةينعم ةمدخ داريتساب همايق دنع ه تاذل ةبيرضلل
Concerned Service.3 Consequently, the Taxable ةبيرضلا باستحا نع ًلاوؤسم ةبيرضلل ع ضاخلا نوكي
Person is responsible to account for the Due Tax1 ةميق نع رارقلإاو ،ةاقلتملا ةمدخلا كلت ىلع 1ةقحتسملا
on the Service received, and report the value and نم )3( ةناخلا يف اهب ةطبترملا تاجرخملا ةبيرضو ة مدخلا
related Output Tax in Box 3 of its VAT return for the ةيبيرضلا ةرتفلا نع هب صّ اخلا ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض رارقإ
Tax Period during which the date of supply of the .ةينعملا ةمدخلا ديروت خيرات اهيف عقي يتلا
Concerned Service crystallises.
Issuing tax invoices ةيبيرضلا ريتاوفلا رادصإ
Registrants are required to issue and deliver an اهميلستو ةيلصأ ةيبيرض ةروتاف رادصإ نيلجسملا ىلع نيعتي
original Tax Invoice to the Recipient of Services عضاخ ديروتب مايقلا نم ً اموي 14 للاخ تامدخلا يقلتمل
within 14 days of making a Taxable Supply.2 2.ةبيرضلل
Since the Registrant is regarded as supplying the ةينعملا ةمدخلا ديروتب موقي هنأ ىلع لجس مُ لل رظنُي هنأ امب
Concerned Service to itself, it is required to issue ةحيحص ةيبيرض ةروتاف ميلستو رادصإ هيلع نيعتيف ،هتاذل
and deliver a valid Tax Invoice to itself in respect of .اهاقلتي ةينعم ةمدخ لك نع هتاذ ىلإ
each Concerned Service received.
Article 59(7)(b) of the Executive Regulation ةحئلالا نم )59( ةداملا نم )7( دنبلا نم )ب( ةرقفلا صّ نت
provides that, where there are (or will be) )رفاوتتس وأ( اهيف رفاوتت يتلا تلااحلا يف هنأ ىلع ةيذيفنتلا
sufficient records available to establish the تاديروتلا نم ةئف وأ ديروت يأ تانايب تابثلإ ةيفاك تلاجس
particulars of any supply or class of supplies, and ةيبيرض ةروتاف ميلست وأ /و رادصإ يلمعلا ريغ نم ناكو
that it would be impractical to require the issuance رادصلإ ةجاحلا مدع ررقت نأ ةئيهلل زوجي ،لجسملا لبق نم
3/9

and/or delivery of a Tax Invoice by the Registrant, .ةددحم تلااح يف ةيبيرض ةروتاف
the FTA may determine that a Tax Invoice is not
required to be issued in certain cases.
Considering the administrative burden to issue Tax مايق ببسب بترتملا يرادلإا ءبعلا رابتعلاا يف ذخلأا عم
Invoices to itself in the case of Concerned Services, تامدخلا ةلاح يف هتاذل ةيبيرض ريتاوف رادصإب لجسملا
the FTA accepts that the Recipient is not required ةروتاف ر ادصإب يقلتملا موقي لا نأ ةئيهلا لبقت ،ةينعملا
to issue a Tax Invoice to itself in respect of ىلع لصح اذإ ةينعملا تامدخلاب قلعتي اميف هتاذل ةيبيرض
Concerned Services if it obtains and retains the ظفتحيو ةلودلا جراخ دوجوملا دروملا نم ةرداصلا ةروتافلا
invoice issued by the overseas supplier reflecting لباقملاو ديروتلا ليصافت ةروتافلا هذه نمضتت نأو اهب
the details and the Consideration paid for the يقلتملا موقي نأ طرشب كلذو ،ةينعملا ةم دخلا ءاقل عوفدملا
Concerned Service, provided that the Recipient
ةيلآ بجومب حيحصلا ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض غلبم باستحاب
accounts for the correct VAT amount under the
ظفتحي نأو ةينعملا ةمدخلا كلت ب قلعتي اميف يسكعلا باستحلاا
reverse charge mechanism in respect thereof and
.تاديروتلا كلت ليصافت تابثلإ ةيفاك تامولعمب
retains sufficient information to establish the
particulars of such supplies.
In exceptional cases, if the supplier did not issue an ىلع ،ةروتاف رادصإب دروملا مقي مل اذإ ،ةيئانثتسا تلااح يف
invoice, e.g. in the case of reinsurance services, a ىلإ رظنُيس ،نيمأتلا ةداعإ تامدخ ةلاح يف لاثملا ليبس
document (or a combination thereof) that reflects لقلأا ىلع نمضتي يذلا )تادنتسملا ةعومجم وأ( دنتسملا
at least the following particulars would be :دروملا ةروتاف هنأ ىلع ةيتلآا ليصافتلا
regarded as the supplier’s invoice:
• the name and address of the supplier of the يبنجأ نايك لثم ،ةينعملا تامدخلا دروم ناونعو مسا •
Concerned Service, e.g. a foreign entity . ةلودلا جراخ نم تامدخلا ديروتب موقي
supplying the Service from outside the UAE,
• the name and address of the Recipient, .يقلتملا ناونعو مسا •
• the date the document was issued, .دنتسملا رادصإ خيرات •
• the date the Service ended, . ةمدخلا ميدقت ءاهتنا خيرات •
• description of the Service supplied, . ةدروملا ةمدخلا فصو •
• Consideration for the supply, including the طورشو ةلصلا تاذ ةلمعلا كلذ يف امب ،ديروتلا لباقم •
relevant currency and, where applicable, .ءاضتقلاا بسحب ،دادسلا
4/9

payment terms.
The above administrative exception does not apply يتلا تلااحلا يف هلاعأ روكذملا يرادلإا ءانثتسلاا قبطُي لا
in instances where the overseas supplier does not و أ ةروتاف ة لودلا جراخ دوجوملا دروملا اهيف ردصي لا
issue an invoice or combination of documents that يف .ةروتاف ةباثمب ةعمتجم ربتعت يتلا تادنتسملا ةعومجم
are collectively regarded to be an invoice. In such ىلع لوصحلل بلط ميدقت يقلتملل زوجي ،تلااحلا هذه
instances the Recipient may apply for an ةداملا نم )7( دنبلا تابلطتم ءافيتسا لاح يف يرادإ ءانثتسا
administrative exception if the requirements of .ةيذيفنتلا ةحئلالا نم )59(
Article 59(7) of the Executive Regulation are met.
Input Tax recovery تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتسا
Registrants are eligible to recover Input Tax to the ردقلاب تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتسلا نيلهؤم نولجسملا نوكي
extent the Concerned Services are used (or are نودصقي وأ( ةينعملا تامدخلا مادختساب هيف نوموقي يذلا
intended to be used) to make Taxable Supplies,6 ةطيرش 6،ةبيرضلل ةعضاخ تاديروتب مايقلل )اهمادختسا
provided the relevant supporting documents are ظافتحلااو ةلصلا تاذ ةمعادلا تادنتسملا ىلع لوصحلا
obtained and retained.7 7.اهب
The Registrant may recover the Input Tax in the ةرتفلا يف تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتسا لجسمُ لل زوجي
first Tax Period (or the immediately following Tax )ًةرشابم اهل ةيلاتلا ةيبيرضلا ةرتفلا يف وأ( ىلولأا ةيبيرضلا
Period) in which the Registrant obtained the ةلصلا تاذ معادلا دنتسملا ىلع لجسملا اهيف لصح يتلا
relevant supporting document (e.g. Tax Invoice or دروملا نم ةرداصلا ةروتافلا وأ ةيبيرضلا ةروتافلا لثم(
invoice issued by the overseas supplier) and paid 8.لباقملا اهيف عفدو )ةلودلا جراخ دوجوملا
the Consideration. 8
Hence, the Recipient would be eligible to recover ةبيرض دادرتسلا ًلاهؤم يقلتملا نوكي ،كلذ ىلع ءً انبو
Input Tax even if it did not issue a Tax Invoice to ،هتاذل ةيبيرض ةروتاف رادصإب مقي مل اذإ ىتح تلاخدملا
itself, provided that it retains the invoice issued by دوجوملا دروملا نم ةرداصلا ةروتافلاب ظفتحي نأ ةطيرش
the overseas supplier (or combination of ةعمتجم ربتعت يتلا تادنتسملا ةعومجم وأ( ةلودلا جراخ
documents that are collectively regarded to be .)ةروتافلا هذه ةباثمب
such invoice).
For Input Tax recovery purposes, the Recipient is ىلع يقلتملا ىلإ رظنُي ،تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتسا ضارغلأ
regarded as having made payment if it pays or ل لاخ هعفد دصقي ن اك وأ لباقملا عفدب ماق اذإ دادسلاب ماق هنأ
5/9

intends to pay the Consideration within six months نم دٍ يزمل 9.هيلع قفتملا د ادسلا خيرات ن م رهشأ ةتس
of the agreed date for payment.9 For more ،تلاخدملا ةبيرض دادرتسلا ينمزلا راطلإا لوح تامولعملا
information on the timeframe for recovering Input .)VATP017( مّ اعلا حيضوتلا ىلإ عوجرلا ىجرُي
Tax, please refer to the Public Clarification
(VATP017).
This Public Clarification issued by the FTA is meant to clarify ىلإ بئارضلل ةيداحتلاا ةئيهلا نع رداصلا ماعلا حيضوتلا اذه فدهي
certain aspects related to the application of Federal Decree- مقر يداحتا نوناقب موسرملا قيبطتب ةقلعتملا عيضاوملا ضعب حيضوت
Law No. 8 of 2017 on Value Added and its Executive ةيذيفنتلا هتحئلاو ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأش يف 2017 ةنسل )8(
Regulation, and their amendments
.امهتلايدعتو
This Public Clarification states the position of the FTA and لدّعُي لاو بئارضلل ةيداحتلاا ةئيهلا فقوم نمضتي ماعلا حيضوتلا اذه
neither amends nor seeks to amend any provision of the اذه نإف ، كلذلو .ةروكذملا تاعيرشتلا ماكحأ نم يأ ليدعت ىلإ فدهي لاو
aforementioned legislation. Therefore, it is effective as of the ةيبيرضلا تاعيرشتلا قيبطت خيرات نم اًرابتعا يراس ماعلا حيضوتلا
date of implementation of the relevant legislation, unless .صنلا يف كلذ فلاخ ديدحت متي مل ام ،ةروكذملا
stated otherwise.
Legislative References: ةيعيرشتلا عجارملا
In this clarification, Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017 on Value ةنسل )8( مقر يداحتا نوناقب موسرملا ىلإ راشُي ،حيضوتلا اذه يف
Added Tax and its amendments is referred to as “Decree-Law” موسرملا" ةرابعب هتلايدعتو ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأش يف 2017
and Cabinet Decision No. 52 of 2017 on the Executive
نأش يف 2017 ةنسل )52( مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق ىلإو ،"نوناقب
Regulation of the Federal Decree-Law No. 8 of 2017 on Value
نأش يف 2017 ةنسل )8( مقر يداحتا نوناقب موسرملل ةيذيفنتلا ةحئلالا
Added Tax, and its amendments, is referred to as “Executive

FTA Organises "Tax Hackathon" to Foster
Innovation in the Tax Sector Through
Artificial Intelligence Technologies
As part of the UAE Innovation Month (UAE Innovates 2025) and the
Year of the Community, the Federal Tax Authority organized the
InnoTAX 2025 AI Hackathon. Held over two days, at Al Shindagha
Museum in Dubai, the hackathon aims to develop innovative
technological solutions to address challenges in the tax sector while
fostering collaboration and innovation between the public and private
sectors.
To read the press release, click here

رﺎﻜﺘﺑﻻا ﻊﻳﺮﺴﺗ" لﻮﺣ ﺔﻛﺮﺘﺸﻣُ ﻞﻤﻋ ﺔﺷرو ﺐﺋاﺮﻀﻠﻟ ﺔﻳدﺎﺤﺗﻻا ﺔﺌﻴﻬﻟا تﺪﻘﻋ ؛ﺮﻳاﺮﺒﻓ
ﺔﺼﺼﺨﺘﻤُ ﻟا ﺔﻴﻤﻟﺎﻌﻟا "ﺖﻔﺳوﺮﻜﻳﺎﻣ" ﺔﻛﺮﺷ ﻊﻣ نوﺎﻌﺘﻟﺎﺑ "ﻲﻋﺎﻨﻄﺻﻻا ءﺎﻛﺬﻟا ماﺪﺨﺘﺳﺎﺑ
.بﻮﺳﺎﺤﻟا تﺎﻴﻨﻘﺗ لﺎﺠﻣ ﻲﻓ

Joint Workshop held by the Federal Tax
Authority and Microsoft on "Accelerating
Innovation with AI"
As part of its participation in UAE Innovation Month 2025, which runs
throughout February, the Federal Tax Authority (FTA) held a joint
workshop on "Accelerating Innovation with AI" in collaboration with
Microsoft, a global leader in computing technologies and the world’s
largest software maker.

FTA intensifies its efforts to raise awareness
about the importance of Corporate Tax
registration
The Federal Tax Authority (FTA) has intensified its awareness efforts to
raise awareness of the need for natural persons who are subject to
corporate tax to submit their corporate tax registration applications,
before the end of March 2025, to avoid violating tax legislation and
incurring administrative penalties.

Federal Tax Authority calls on registrants to
benefit from ‘grace period’ up to end of
March to update tax records to avoid
administrative penalties
The Federal Tax Authority (FTA) has called on registrants who have yet
to update their tax records to take advantage of an extended ‘grace
period’ to submit their tax records, stating that submissions received
before the end of March 2025 will not incur any administrative
penalties.

text:
ﺐـﺋاﺮﻀـﻟا ﻲـﻌﻓاد ةﺮــﺸﻧ
Taxpayers Bulletin
ﺐــﺋاﺮﻀﻠﻟ ﺔــﻳدﺎﺤﺗﻻا ﺔــﺌﻴﻬﻟاو ﺔــﻴﻟﺎﻤﻟا ةرازو
ﺔــﻣاﺮﻏ ﻦــﻣ ءﺎــﻔﻋ(cid:2)ﻟ ةردﺎــﺒﻣ قﻼﻃإ ﻦــﻋ نﺎــﻨﻠﻌﺗ
تﺎﻛﺮﺸﻟا ﺔﺒﻳﺮﺿ ضاﺮﻏ… ﻞﻴﺠﺴﺘﻟا ﻲﻓ ﺮﺧﺄﺘﻟا
قﻼﻃإ نﺄﺷ ﻲﻓ ءارزﻮﻟا ﺲﻠﺠﻣ راﺮﻗ روﺪﺻ ﻦﻋ ﺐﺋاﺮﻀﻠﻟ ﺔﻳدﺎﺤﺗﻻا ﺔﺌﻴﻬﻟاو ﺔﻴﻟﺎﻤﻟا ةرازو ﺖﻨﻠﻋأ

تﺎﺌﻔﻟا هﺬﻫ ﻢﻳﺪﻘﺗ ﺔﻄﻳﺮﺷ ،ةدﺪﺤﻤﻟا ﺔﻠﻬﻤﻟا لﻼﺧ ﻲﺒﻳﺮﻀﻟا ﻞﻴﺠﺴﺘﻟا ﺐﻠﻃ ﻢﻳﺪﻘﺗ ﻲﻓ
ءﺎﻬﺘﻧا ﺦﻳرﺎﺗ ﻦﻣ ﺮﻬﺷأ 7 زوﺎﺠﺘﺗ ﻻ ةﺮﺘﻓ لﻼﺧ ،لﺎﺤﻟا ﺐﺴﺤﺑ يﻮﻨﺴﻟا ﺢﻳﺮﺼﺘﻟا وأ ﻲﺒﻳﺮﻀﻟا راﺮﻗšا
.تﺎﻛﺮﺸﻟا ﺔﺒﻳﺮﺿ نﻮﻧﺎﻗ مﺎﻜﺣŠ ±ﻘﻓوو ،ﻰﻟوŠا ﺔﻴﺒﻳﺮﻀﻟا ﻢﻬﺗﺮﺘﻓ
ﺎﻨﻫ ﻂﻐﺿا ،ﻲﻔﺤﺼﻟا ﺮﺒﺨﻟا ةءاﺮﻘﻟ
MoF and Federal Tax Authority launch
initiative to waive late registration penalties
under the Corporate Tax Law
The Ministry of Finance and the Federal Tax Authority have announced
the issuance of a Cabinet Decision launching an initiative to waive
administrative penalties for corporate taxpayers and certain exempt
persons who failed to submit their tax registration applications with the
FTA within the required timeframe.
To qualify, eligible parties must file their tax return or annual statements
within a period not exceeding seven (7) months from the end of their
first tax period, as stipulated under the Corporate Tax Law.
To read the press release, click here

FTA and UAE Media Council Raise Tax
Awareness Among Influencers
The Federal Tax Authority (FTA), in collaboration with the UAE Media
Council and in the presence of representatives from the New Media
Academy, has held an awareness session for content creators and
influencers on the topic of tax compliance in the UAE. These sessions
are part of the FTA’s ongoing and wider efforts to highlight tax
compliance requirements for corporate tax and VAT, in the UAE and
companies eligible to pay UAE federal taxes.

FTA Organises Awareness Workshop on Basics
of VAT
The Federal Tax Authority (FTA), in collaboration with the Sharjah
Chamber of Commerce and Industry, organised an awareness
workshop on the Basics of VAT. FTA’s representatives provided a
comprehensive overview of procedures, regulatory requirements, and
the latest updates related to key sectors such as automotive and
transport within the context of VAT.

FTA Presents Tax Awareness Session for
Students
The Federal Tax Authority presented a tax awareness session for
students at the International School of Creative Science. During the
session, FTA experts shared valuable insights to enhance students'
understanding of taxation. Empowering the next generation with
knowledge is key to building a brighter future.

As part of its commitment to supporting stakeholders in the tax sector,

title: Gold and Diamonds – Amendment to Tax Treatment of Making Service | VATP032
text:
VATP032
VAT Public Clarification ة فاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأشب ماع حيضوت
Gold and Diamonds – Amendment to Tax ةمدخل ةيبيرضلا ةلماعملا ليدعت – سامللأاو بهذلا
Treatment of Making Service ةيعنصملا
Please be informed that Public Clarification لحم لحي VATP032 ماعلا حيضوتلا نأب ملعلا ىجري
VATP032 replaces Public Clarification VATP029.
. VATP029 ماعلا حيضوتلا
Please be informed that this Public Clarification ه لحم لح VATP032 ماعلا حيضوتلا نأب ملعلا ىجري
VATP032 was replaced by Public Clarification
. VATP043 ماعلا حيضوتلا
VATP043.
Issue عوضوملا
Jewellers generally receive a consideration for the تلاوغشم ديروت ريظن ًلاباقم ةغاصلا ىضاقتي ،مّ اع لكشب
supply of gold and diamond jewellery to cover the لباقم كلذكو سامللأاو بهذلا رعس ةيطغتل سامللأاو بهذلا
price of the gold and diamond as well as the . ةيعنصملا ةمدخ
Making Service.
In some instances, taxable persons supplying gold نودروي نيذلا ةبيرضلل نوعضاخلا موقي ،تلااحلا ضعب يف
or diamond jewellery reflect the gold or diamond سامللأا وأ بهذلا رعس جاردإب سامللأا وأ بهذلا تلاوغشم
price and the Making Charges separately on the tax ةيبيرضلا ةروتافلا يف لصفنم لكشب ةيعنصملا رعسو
invoice issued for the supply, and in other cases دحاو رعسك اهجاردإب نوموقي ىرخأ تلااح يفو ،ةرداصلا
both are reflected as a single price without .نوكم لكل ةلصفنملا راعسلأا نع حاصفلإا نود
disclosing the separate prices of each component.
This Public Clarification replaces VATP029 وينوي يف روشنملا VATP029 ماعلا حيضوتلاب لدبتس ي
published in June 2022, and provides guidance on قيبطت نأشب تاداشرإ مدقيو ،ماعلا حيضوتلا اذه 2022
the application of the VAT legislation with regards ةيعنصملا رعسب قلعتي اميف ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض تاعيرشت
to the Making Charges received by jewellers in سلجم رارق ىلع ليدعتلل ًاقفو ،ةغاصل ا هاضاقتي يذلا
accordance with the amendment to Cabinet ةبيرض قيبطت ةيلآ نأش يف 2018 ةنسل )25( مقر ءارزولا
Decision No. 25 of 2018 on the Mechanism of يف نيلجسملا نيب سامللأاو بهذلا ىلع ةفاضملا ةميقلا
1/12

Applying Value Added Tax on Gold and Diamonds .)")25( مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق"( ةلودلا
between Registrants in the State (“Cabinet
Decision No. 25”).
Terms Used in this Public Clarification ماعلا حيضوتلا اذه يف ةمدختسم تاحلطصم
For the purposes of this Public Clarification, the ةيلاتلا تاحلطصملا ىلإ راشي ،ماعلا حيضوتلا اذه ضارغلأ
terms below shall have the meaning assigned :اهنم لك نيرق ةدراولا يناعملا
against each:
• “Gold”: gold and/or diamonds. . سامللأا وأ/و بهذلا :" بهذلا" •
• “Making Service”: the service of .بهذلا تاجتنم عينصت ةمدخ :"ةيعنصملا ةمدخ" •
making/manufacturing Gold Items.
• “Making Charge”: the price charged by لباقم ةغاصلا هضرفي يذلا رعسلا :" ةيعنصملا رعس" •
jewellers in return for making/manufacturing .بهذلا تاجتنم عينصت
Gold Items.

6. Article 4(1) of the Executive Regulation states that, where ماق اذإ هنأ ىلع ةيذيفنتلا ةحئلالا نم )4( ةداملا نم )1( دنبلا صّ ني .6

multiple supplies.

title: Application of the Reverse Charge Mechanism on Precious Metals and Precious Stones between Registrants in the State for the purposes of Value Added Tax | VATP043
text:
VATP043
VAT Public Clarification ة فاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأشب ماع حيضوت

Precious Metals and Precious Stones between ضارغلأ ةلودلا يف نيلجسملا نيب ةميركلا راجحلأاو

Added Tax

VATP043 replaces Public Clarification VATP032. . VATP032 ماعلا حيضوتلا

No. 127 of 2024 is 26 February 2025. . 2025 رياربف 26 وه 2024 ةنسل )127(
Issue عوضوملا
Cabinet Decision No. 127 of 2024 on the نأشب 2024 ةنسل )127( مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق ردص

Added Tax (“Cabinet Decision No. 127 of 2024”) يف 2018 ةنسل )25( مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق لحم لحيل
was issued to replace Cabinet Decision No. 25 of سامللأاو بهذلا ىلع ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض قيبطت ةيلآ نأش
2018 on the Mechanism of Applying Value Added )25( مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق"( ةلودلا يف نيلجسملا نيب
Tax on Gold and Diamonds between Registrants in
.)"2018 ةنسل
the State (“Cabinet Decision No. 25 of 2018”).
Cabinet Decision No. 127 of 2024 expands the قاطن 2024 ةنسل )127( مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق عسّ وُيو
scope of Goods on which the domestic reverse ل مشتل يلحملا يسكعلا باستحلاا ةيلآ اهيلع قبطُت يتلا علسلا
charge applies to include, in addition to gold, ةعونصملا تارهوجملاو سامللأاو بهذلا ىلإ ةفاضلإاب
diamonds and jewellery made thereof, other ،ةددحم ةميرك راجحأو ىرخأ ةنيمث نداعم ،امهنم
specified precious metals, precious stones and .اهنم ةعونصملا تارهوجملاو
jewellery made thereof.
This Public Clarification explains the changes to ىلع اهؤارجإ مت يتلا تارييغتلا ماعلا حيضوتلا اذه نّيبي
1/14

the rules as implemented by Cabinet Decision No. ةنسل )127( مقر ءارزولا سلجم رارق بجومب دعاوقلا
127 of 2024. The Cabinet Decision is effective from 26 خيرات نم ًارابتعا ءارزولا سلجم رارقب لمعُيو .2024
26 February 2025. . 2025 رياربف
Summary صخلم
The list of Goods1 within the scope of the domestic باستحلاا ةيلآ قاطن يف لخدت يتلا 1علسلا ةمئاق عيسوت مت
reverse charge has been expanded to include ةميركلا راجحلأاو ةنيمثلا نداعملا لمشتل يلحملا يسكعلا
precious metals, precious stones and jewellery ةميق نوكت نأ ىلع ،امهنم يأ نم ةعونصملا تارهوجملاو
made thereof, provided that the value of such ةميق نم ىلعأ ةميركلا راجحلأا وأ ةنيمثلا نداعملا كلت
precious metals or precious stones is higher than .)"ةنيمثلا علسلا"( ىرخلأا تانوكملا
the value of other components (“Precious
Goods”).
The specified precious metals are gold, silver, مويدلابلاو ةضفلاو بهذلا يه ةددح مُ لا ةنيمثلا نداعملا
palladium and platinum. .نيتلابلاو
The specified precious stones are diamonds يعيبطلا( سامللأا يه ةددح مُ لا ةميركلا راجحلأا
(natural and manufactured/synthetic), pearls, . درمزلاو ريفزلاو توقايلاو ؤلؤللاو )يعانطصلاا/عّنصمُ لاو
rubies, sapphires and emeralds.
The following conditions must be satisfied for ع لسلا ديروت ىلع يلحملا يسكعلا باستحلاا ةيلآ قيبطتل
application of the domestic reverse charge on the :ةيتلآا طورشلا ءافيتسا بجي ،ةنيمثلا
supply of Precious Goods:
• The Recipient of the Precious Goods ًلاجسمُ " ملتسملا" ةنيمثلا علسلا ملتسم نوكي نأ بجي •
(“Recipient”) must be registered for VAT in the . ةلودلا يف ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرضل
UAE.
• The Recipient must intend to resell the جاتنلإ اهلامعتسا وأ اه عيب ةداعإ ملتسملا دصقي نأ بجي •
Precious Goods or use them to produce or .ةنيمثلا علسلا عينصت وأ
manufacture Precious Goods.
• The Recipient must provide a written حيرصت ميدقت ملتسملا ىلع بجي ،ديروتلا خيرات لبق •
declaration before the date of supply :يتأي ام دكؤي يطخ
2/14

confirming the following:
o the Recipient is registered for VAT in the يف ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرضل لجسم ملتسملا نأ o
UAE, and و ،ة لودلا
o the Recipient intends to resell the Precious جاتنلإ اهلامعتسا وأ اهعيب ةداعإ دصقي ملتسملا نأ o
Goods or use them in the production or .ةنيمثلا علسلا عينصت وأ
manufacturing of Precious Goods.
• The supplier must, before the date of supply, ظفحو ملاتسا د رّ و مُ لا ىلع بجي ،ديروتلا خيرات لبق •
receive and keep the declaration from the نأ نم ققحتلاو ملتسملا نم هملتسا يذلا حيرصتلا
Recipient and verify that the Recipient is لئاسولل ًاقفو( ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرضل لجّ سم ملتسملا
registered for VAT (via the FTA’s approved .)نأشلا اذه يف ةئيهلا ىدل ةدمتعملا
means).
The application of the reverse charge mechanism علسلا ىلع رصحني يسكعلا باستحلاا ةيلَا قيبطت •
is restricted to the supply of the Precious Goods عينصتلا/ةيعنصملا تامدخ لمشي لاو ،طقف ةنيمثلا
and does not extend to Services for the دحاو ديروت نم ًاءزج تامدخلا ربتعت مل ام ،تارهوجملل
making/manufacturing of jewellery, unless the .ةنيمث علسل بكرم
Services are part of a single composite supply of
Precious Goods.
Detailed analysis يليصفت ليلحت
Expansion of the scope of the domestic reverse ةنيمثلا نداعملا لمشيل يلحملا يسكعلا باستحلاا قاطن عيسوت

title: Value of supply - Barter Transactions | VATP042
text:
VATP042
VAT Public Clarification ة فاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأشب ماع حيضوت
Value of supply - Barter Transactions ةضياقملا تلاماعم – ديروتلا ةميق
Issue عوضوملا
In general, taxable persons1 receive monetary ريظن يدقن 1لباقم 1ةبيرضلل نوعضاخلا ىقلتي ،مّ اع لكشب
consideration1 for the supplies they make but لباقملا اوملتسي نأ ًاضيأ نكمي نكلو ،اهب موقي يتلا تاديروتلا
consideration may also be received in the form of .يدقن ريغ لباقم يّ أ ،تامدخ وأ علس لكش يف
goods or services, i.e. non-monetary
consideration.
Transactions which involve the exchange of goods تامدخلا وأ/و علسلل لدابت نمضتت يتلا تلاماعملا فرعُت
and/or services, are known as barter transactions. .ةضياقملا تلاماعمب
This Public Clarification clarifies the VAT treatment ةميقلا ةبيرض ةلماعم نايب ىلإ مّ اعلا حيضوتلا اذه فدهي
of barter transactions. .ةضياقملا تلاماعمل ةفاضملا
Summary صخلم
Barter transactions involve at least two supplies, لك نأ يأ ،لقلأا ىلع نيديروت ةضياقملا تلاماعم نمضتت
i.e. each party makes a supply to the other. .رخلآا فرطلل ديروتب موقي فرط
Such supplies are subject to the general VAT rules, ةميقلا ةبيرضل ةماعلا دعاوقلل تاديروتلا هذه عضخت
nevertheless, special valuation rules apply. هذه ةميق ديدحتل ةصاخ دعاوق قبطُ ت نكلو ،ةفاضملا
.تاديروتلا
Generally, the value of the supply is the غلبم هنم ًاحورطم لباقملا يه ديروتلا ةميق نوكت ،مَ اع لكشب
consideration less the tax amount. . ةبيرضلا
The value of supply within a barter transaction is ةيقوسلا ةميقلا يه ةضياقم ةلماعم يف ديروتلا ةميق نوكت
the market value of the non-monetary غلبم هنم ًاحورطم دروملا هاقلتي يذلا يدقنلا ريغ لباقملل
consideration received by a supplier excluding the . ةبيرضلا
tax amount.
Where a supplier receives both monetary and نوكت ،يدقن ريغ ً لاباقمو ً ا يدقن ً لاباقم دروملا ىقلت لاح يف
non-monetary consideration, the value of supply ةيقوسلا ةميقلا هيلإ ًافاضم ي دقنلا ءزجلا يه ديروتلا ةميق
1/9

is the monetary part plus the market value of the . ةبيرضلا غلبم هنم ًاحورطم ،لباقملا نم يدقنلا ريغ ءزجلل
non-monetary part of the consideration, excluding
the tax amount.
Detailed analysis يليصفت ليلحت
VAT Treatment ة فاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض ةلماعم
The VAT treatment of a barter transaction is the يه ةضياقملا ةلماعمل ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض ةلماعم نوكت
same as that of a supply made for monetary لباقم ريظن هب مايقلا مت ديروت ىلع ةقبطمُ لا ةلماعملا تاذ
consideration. .يدقن
For VAT purposes, the differences between a نيب تافلاتخلاا لثمتت ،ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض ضارغلأ
barter and non-barter transaction are that a ةضياقملا ساسأ ىلع موقت لا يتلا ةلماعملاو ةضياقملا ةلماعم
barter transaction includes at least two supplies نأ يأ( لقلأا ىلع نيديروت نمضتت ةضياقملا ةلماعم نأ يف
(i.e. each party makes at least one supply to the ،)رخلآا فرطلل دحاو ديروتب لقلأا ىلع موقي فرط لك
other party), and special valuation rules apply. .تاديروتلا ةميق ديدحتل ةصاخ دعاوق اهيف قبطُتو
Each party within a barter transaction must مييقت ةضياقملا ةلماعم فارطأ نم فرط لك ىلع بجي
evaluate the VAT treatment of the goods or موقي يتلا تامدخلا وأ علسلل ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض ةلماعم
services they are providing. A supply within a ًايأ ةضياقم ةلماعم نمض ديروتلا نوكي نأ نكميو .اهديروتب
barter transaction can be: :يتأي امم
• A taxable supply1 subject to VAT at the ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرضل عضخي 1ةبيرضلل عضاخ ديروت •
standard rate of 5%. .)%5( ةيساسلأا ةبسنلاب
• A taxable supply subject to VAT at the zero- ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرضل عضخي ةبيرضلل عضاخ ديروت •
rate if all the relevant requirements for zero- قيبطت تابلطتم عيمج ءافيتسا مت لاح يف ،رفصلا ةبسنب
rating are met. . ةلصلا تاذ رفصلا ةبسن
• A supply exempt from VAT if all the relevant مت لاح يف ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نم ىفعمُ ديروت •
requirements for exemption are met. . ةلصلا تاذ ءافعلإا تابلطتم عيمج ءافيتسا
• A supply outside the scope of UAE VAT, e.g. if ،ةلودلا يف ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض قاطن جراخ ديروت •
the place of supply is outside the UAE. . ةلودلا جراخ ديروتلا ناكم ناك اذإ لاثملا ليبس ىلع
2/9

Valuation of the supply ديروتلا مييقت
To the extent the consideration for a supply is ريظن لباقملا ناك اذإ ةضياقم ةلماعم ةباثمب ديروتلا نوكي لا
monetary, such supply is not a barter transaction. .ًايدقن ديروتلا اذه
To the extent the consideration is non-monetary, ،يدقن ريغ لباقملا ناك اذإ ةضياقم ةلماعم ةلماعملا ل كّ شُتو
the transaction constitutes a barter transaction, ريظن ىقلتم يدقن لباقم يأ عومجم يه ديروتلا ةميق نوكتو
and the value of the supply is the sum of any ،لباقملا نم يدقنلا ريغ ءزجلل ةيقوسلا ةميقلاو ديروتلا
monetary consideration received for the supply 2.ةبيرضلا غلبم هنم ًاحورطم
and the market value of the non-monetary part of
the consideration, excluding the tax amount.2
To determine the market value of the non- قبطُت ،لباقملا نم يدقنلا ريغ ءزجلل ةيقوسلا ةميقلا ديدحتل
monetary part of the consideration, the following :ةيتلآا ئدابملا
principles apply:3
1. The market value of a supply of goods or لباقملا يه تامدخ وأ علس ديروتل ةيقوسلا ةميقلا نوكت .1
services is the monetary consideration the هديروت مت اذإ ماع لكشب ديروتلا هققحيس ناك يذلا يدقنلا
supply would generally achieve if supplied in هنأ يأ ،ةلودلا يف خيراتلا تاذ يف ةهباشم فورظ يف
similar circumstances at that date in the UAE, نيطبترم ريغ صاخشأ نيب رح لكشب هب مايقلا متي ً اديروت
being a supply freely made between persons .لكش يأب
who are not connected in any manner.
For example, a VAT-registered social media لصاوتلا تاصنم دحأ ىلع رثؤم موقي ،لاثملا ليبس ىلع
influencer supplies marketing services with a ديروتب ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرضل ًلاجسم يعامتجلاا
market value of AED 1,000 (inclusive of VAT) ةل ماش( مهرد 1,000 غلبت ةيقوس ةميقب ةيقيوست تامدخ
to a restaurant in the UAE in exchange for غ لبم ريظن ةلودلا يف معطم ىلإ )ةفاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض
AED 900 and a meal valued at AED 100 ةبيرض ةلماش( مهرد 100 اهتميق ةبجوو مهرد 900
(inclusive of VAT) from the restaurant. .معطملا نم ةمدقمُ )ةفاضملا ةميقلا

This Public Clarification states the position of the FTA and لاو لدّعُي لاو بئارضلل ةيداحتلاا ةئيهلا فقوم نمضتي ماعلا حيضوتلا اذه

stated otherwise.

2. Article 34 of the Decree-Law states that the value of ديروتلا ةميق نوكت نأ ىلع نوناقب موسرملا نم )34( ةداملا صّ نت .2

follows:

3. If the services were received by the taxable person يذلا ةبيرضلل عضاخلا لبق نم ةملتسملا تامدخلا تناك اذإ .3

3. Article 25(2), (3), and (4) of the Executive Regulation ةيذيفنتلا ةحئلالا نم )25( ةداملا نم )4(و )3(و )2( دونبلا صّ نت .3

title: Crypto currency Mining | VATP039
text:
VATP039
VAT Public Clarification ة فاضملا ةميقلا ةبيرض نأشب ماع حيضوت
Crypto currency mining ةيمقرلا تلامعلا نيدعت
Issue عوضوملا
With the rising popularity of cryptocurrencies, the ةلماعملا ةلأسم تحبصأ ،ةيمقرلا تلامعلاب مامتهلاا ديازت عم
question of tax treatment of crypto currency . ةديازتم ةيمهأ تاذ ةيمقرلا تلامعلا نيدعتل ةيبيرضلا
mining has become increasingly relevant.
In a case of proof of work, a person can mine crypto نيدعتب صخشلا موقي نأ نكمملا نم ،"لمعلا تابثإ" لاح يف
currency for his own account, or for someone’s نم رخآ صخش باسحل وأ ،صاخلا هباسحل ةيمقر ةلمع
account by providing access to excess عرازم وأ ةقئاف ةيبسوح ةردق ىلإ لوصولا ريفوت للاخ
computational power or data farms. .تانايب
For purposes of this public clarification, “crypto ًلاكش "ةيمقرلا تلامعلا" د عُت ، م اعلا حيضوتلا اذه ضارغلأ
currencies” are a form of virtual assets.1 Examples تلامعلا ةلثمأ نمضتت 1.ةيضارتفلاا لوصلأا لاكشأ نم
of crypto currencies include Bitcoin, Ethereum تلامعلاو )يكيسلاكلا( مويرثي لاو نيوكتيبلا ةيمقرلا
(Classic), and other currencies that are based on ." لمعلا تابثإ" ىلإ دنتست يتلا ىرخلأا
proof of work.
This Public Clarification clarifies the VAT treatment ةميقلا ةبيرض ةلماعم نايب ىلإ م اعلا حيضوتلا اذه فدهي
of crypto currency mining using the proof-of-work ةيلآ مادختساب ةيمقرلا تلامعلا نيدعت تلاماعمل ةفاضملا
mechanism. .لمعلا تابثإ
Summary صخلم
Crypto currency mining by a person2 for his own صاخلا هباسحل ةيمقرلا تلامعلا نيدعتب 2صخشلا مايق د عُي لا
account is not a taxable supply3 and falls outside ةميقلا ةبيرض قاطن جراخ عقي لب 3ةبيرضلل ًاعضاخ ًاديروت
the scope of VAT. . ةفاضملا
Mining crypto currency on behalf of another يأ ،رخآ صخش نع َةباين ةيمقرلا تلامعلا نيدعت ربتعي
person, i.e. supplying computational power, is . ةبيرضلل عضاخ تامدخل ديروت ةباثمب ،ةيبسوح ةردق ديروت
considered to be a taxable supply of services.
